{"id":"COS-rst","title":"Migrate gas2mqtt example to framework persistence","description":"Phase 5: Update gas2mqtt example to use cosalette.stores instead of its own implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:02:41Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:02:41Z"}

{"id":"COS-cr2","title":"Epic 1: Adapter Lifecycle Protocol","description":"Auto-manage adapters implementing __aenter__/__aexit__ (context manager protocol). Framework calls lifecycle methods during startup/shutdown automatically — no explicit lifespan= hook needed for the common case. Only context manager protocol supported (not named methods). Lifespan= hook remains for advanced orchestration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:47:19Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:47:19Z"}

{"id":"COS-cr2.1","title":"Define adapter lifecycle protocol (context manager)","description":"Add AdapterLifecycle protocol detection to the framework. Implement _is_lifecycle_adapter() helper that checks if a resolved adapter instance supports __aenter__/__aexit__. This is purely the protocol definition and detection — no orchestration changes yet. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:47:28Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:47:28Z"}

{"id":"COS-rg9","title":"Epic 2: Settings-Aware Adapter Constructors","description":"Extend adapter resolution to inspect __init__ type hints and auto-inject Settings (DI). Eliminates factory function boilerplate — adapter classes that type-hint Settings in __init__ get it injected automatically. Not backwards compatible. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:48:16Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:48:16Z"}

{"id":"COS-rg9.1","title":"Extend adapter resolution for DI","description":"Modify _resolve_adapters / _call_factory in _app.py to inspect __init__ signatures of adapter classes and auto-inject Settings (and potentially other registered types like ClockPort). Reuse the same signature-based DI pattern from _injection.py. When a class has __init__(self, settings: MySettings), resolve Settings and inject. No-arg constructors remain supported. Factory callables continue to work as before. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:48:26Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:48:26Z"}

{"id":"COS-kgv","title":"Epic 3: Direct Function Registration","description":"Add imperative app.add_device(), app.add_telemetry(), app.add_command() methods for registering imported functions without pass-through wrappers. Refactor decorators to call these internally (DRY). Mirrors FastAPI's app.add_api_route() pattern. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:03Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:03Z"}

{"id":"COS-kgv.1","title":"Add imperative registration methods","description":"Implement add_device(name, func, *, init=), add_telemetry(name, func, *, interval=, publish=, persist=, init=), add_command(name, func, *, init=) on App class. Refactor existing device(), telemetry(), command() decorators to call these internally for DRY. All validation/registration logic moves to the add_* methods; decorators become thin wrappers. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:12Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:12Z"}

{"id":"COS-67x","title":"Epic 4: Conditional Device Registration","description":"Add enabled= parameter (bool or callable) to all device decorators and imperative add_* methods. When enabled=False (or callable returns False), the device is skipped at registration time. Cleans up imperative if/else blocks around device registration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:47Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:47Z"}

{"id":"COS-67x.1","title":"Add enabled= parameter to device decorators","description":"Add enabled= parameter (bool | Callable[[], bool]) to device(), telemetry(), command() decorators and their add_* imperative counterparts. When enabled=False or callable returns False, skip registration entirely (decorator returns the function unmodified, add_* is a no-op). Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:55Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:49:55Z"}

{"id":"COS-jki","title":"Epic 5: Declarative Adapter Block","description":"Add adapters= dict parameter to App.__init__() for grouped, constructor-level adapter declaration. Accepts dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]]. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls. Benefits from Epic 2's settings-aware DI for the adapter values. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §5","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:50:32Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:50:32Z"}

{"id":"COS-jki.1","title":"Add adapters= constructor parameter","description":"Accept adapters= dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]] in App(). Parse tuples and register via existing adapter() internals. Support both tuple (impl, dry_run) and bare impl value. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls — duplicates raise ValueError.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:50:40Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:50:40Z"}

{"id":"COS-igu.1","title":"Source code consistency audit","description":"Review _app.py, _injection.py, _strategies.py, and any new modules added by Epics 1-6. Ensure consistent naming conventions, parameter ordering, error message style, docstring format. Refactor any inconsistencies so the code reads as one cohesive design.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:52:11Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:52:11Z"}

{"id":"COS-rt0","title":"Signal handler timing gap: install before adapter lifecycle entry","description":"Signal handlers are installed after lifecycle adapters are entered in _run_async(). If SIGTERM/SIGINT arrives during a slow __aenter__, Python's default handler terminates the process without setting shutdown_event and without running adapter/health/MQTT cleanup. Moving _install_signal_handlers() before the async with _enter_lifecycle_adapters() block would close this window, but requires design thought about what happens if shutdown fires during adapter entry (abort remaining entries?). Ref: PR #49 review feedback.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-26T08:53:08Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-26T08:53:08Z"}

{"id":"COS-7ca","title":"Adapter entry timeout/cancellation during shutdown","description":"If a lifecycle adapter __aenter__ hangs indefinitely, setting shutdown_event cannot interrupt it (no cancellation). The process appears stuck. Consider wrapping adapter entry in asyncio.wait_for with a configurable timeout, or using a cancel scope. Filed from COS-rt0 fix — the signal handler timing gap is closed, but a truly hanging __aenter__ remains uninterruptible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-02-26T21:25:08Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-02-26T21:25:08Z"}

old_value: {"id":"COS-5ut","title":"Data model + API surface (group= parameter)","description":"Phase 1: Add group parameter to telemetry registration layer and public API. 1) Add group: str | None = None to _TelemetryRegistration dataclass. 2) Add group param to telemetry() decorator, thread to both paths (named and root). 3) Add group param to add_telemetry() imperative method. 4) Validation: reject empty string group=''. 5) Tests: verify group field stored, validation, backward compat. Acceptance: All existing tests pass. New tests verify group field.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-03-03T17:09:47Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-03-03T17:09:47Z"}

{"id":"COS-91o","title":"Tick-aligned group scheduler (_run_telemetry_group)","description":"Phase 2: Implement the tick-aligned priority-queue scheduler. 1) Create _run_telemetry_group() method with heapq-based scheduler. 2) Integer-millisecond tick arithmetic (_to_ms helper). 3) Per-handler DI resolution, init functions, publish strategies. 4) Per-handler error isolation within batches. 5) Init failure excludes handler from group. 6) Shutdown-aware sleep using first handler DeviceContext. 7) Device store save on shutdown (finally block). 8) Update _start_device_tasks() to partition by group. 9) Unit tests: single handler, same interval, mixed intervals, error isolation, init failure, shutdown, registration order. Acceptance: Grouped handlers coalesce correctly. Ungrouped unchanged.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-03-03T17:09:56Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-03-03T17:09:56Z"}

{"id":"COS-jjp","title":"Edge cases, integration tests + ADR-018","description":"Phase 3: Polish and documentation. 1) Float precision tests (300x12 == 3600 coalesces). 2) Integration tests with AppHarness + FakeClock (grouped+ungrouped coexist, multiple groups, full MQTT verification). 3) Write ADR-018-coalescing-groups.md (promote from planning docs). 4) Update concept docs (architecture, telemetry). 5) Update guides (telemetry-device, adapters). 6) Update API reference. 7) Delete planning docs (coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md, telemetry-coalescing-groups.md, telemetry-session-coalescing.md) after ADR is written. Acceptance: Full test coverage, docs updated, ADR-018 in place.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"fab@tech-kyb.de","created_at":"2026-03-03T17:10:08Z","created_by":"Fabian Koerner","updated_at":"2026-03-03T17:10:08Z"}

new_value: {"description":"_run_telemetry_group computes elapsed time from sleep_ctx.clock.now() (injected ClockPort) but ctx.sleep() uses asyncio.sleep (real monotonic time). With FakeClock, clock.now() never advances during sleeps so elapsed is always 0, causing wait_seconds to be an absolute offset rather than a delta. This is harmless in current tests (short intervals, shutdown after 1-2 ticks) but would break multi-tick deterministic test scenarios. Fix: either advance FakeClock inside ctx.sleep(), or base elapsed calculation on time.monotonic()/loop.time(). See PR #62 review comments from Copilot."}
